trivia_bullets.text: Woe Strider is one of the most-played free sacrifice outlets in Commander, appearing in approximately 81,400 decks on EDHREC (rank 1686). The combination of a zero-mana sacrifice cost, Scry 1 selection, built-in token fodder (a 0/1 Goat on entry), and an Escape clause makes it a format staple in sacrifice-based strategies [EDHREC, Scryfall].

Woe Strider has been reprinted in five Commander precons and supplemental products including Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate (2022), New Capenna Commander (2022), Bloomburrow Commander (2024), Tarkir: Dragonstorm Commander (2025), and Secrets of Strixhaven Commander (2026) — an unusually dense reprint schedule reflecting its consistent demand as a sacrifice-outlet workhorse [Scryfall prints].

Woe Strider is typed as a Horror — not a named Theros creature type like Minotaur or Harpy — signaling it is one of the formless, nameless things that haunt the Underworld's deeper layers rather than a figure from Greek-mythology-adjacent Theran lore [Scryfall]. The goat token it creates may nod to the scapegoat motif: the sacrificed innocent absorbing woe on behalf of the larger creature.

related_cards.name: Viscera Seer
related_cards.note: The free-sacrifice-outlet archetype's baseline; Woe Strider exceeds it via Escape and token

related_cards.name: Cauldron Familiar
related_cards.note: Classic sacrifice-loop partner in Standard-era THB decks
